name: Coffee Toffee Pie
recipeIngredient: 1 Unsweetened chocolate square, melted and cooled
2 tablespoons Instant coffee (try using espresso powder)
recipeInstructions:
1. Make pastry shell: In a medium bowl, combine piecrust mix with brown sugar, walnuts, and grated chocolate.
Add 1 tablespoon water and the vanilla. Using a fork, mix until well blended.
Press into a well-buttered 9-inch pie plate; press firmly against the bottom and sides of the pie plate.
Bake for 15 minutes. Cool on a wire rack.
2. Make filling: In a small bowl, beat the butter with an electric mixer at medium speed until creamy.
Gradually add granulated sugar, beating until light. Blend in the melted chocolate and 2 teaspoons instant coffee.
Add 1 egg, beating for 5 minutes. Add a second egg, beating for another 5 minutes longer.
Pour the filling into the baked pie shell. Refrigerate the pie, covered, at least 8 hours (best held overnight).
3. Topping: The next day, make the topping. In a large bowl, combine cream with 2 tablespoons instant coffee and confectioners' sugar.
Cover and refrigerate for 1 hour.
4. Beat the cream mixture until stiff. Decorate the pie with the topping using a pastry bag with a star or shell decorating tip, if desired.
Garnish with chocolate curls.
5. Refrigerate at least 2 hours before serving.
